response_turn1:
After retrieving and reviewing the full text, here is my assessment:

LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 96
REASON: DailyMed is a drug labeling database maintained by the NIH/NLM (not the FDA directly) that publishes manufacturer-submitted prescribing information — it does not issue its own "reclassification" directives, severity re-tiering bulletins, or prescriber guidance documents; the cited "VIGIL-W post-marketing registry," the reference number "DailyMed-ISR-2026-01," and the §4.2/§4.3 regulatory structure do not correspond to any real DailyMed or FDA publication format, and no such reclassification of warfarin + ibuprofen exists in verified FDA records.
